_comment: Policy YOU (the human) own. The ORCHESTRATOR (the cloud Claude session that spawned the local-offload subagent) reads this to decide how to fulfill the agent's capability-requests. The offload agent does NOT hold write/web tools, so it cannot perform these itself and cannot edit this file to widen its own privileges. Decisions: allow | ask | deny.

write._note: Writes under sandbox/ are auto-approved. Anywhere else: the orchestrator asks you (y/N) before writing. deny_paths are NEVER written on the agent's behalf — they protect the gate's own guardrails.

exfil_guard._note: The QUERY STRING is the exfiltration channel (the model could fold a secret it read into the query). The orchestrator screens every query before running it.
refuse_if_query_looks_like: file contents, secrets, tokens, API keys, or env values
long opaque / base64 / hex blobs (> ~40 chars) rather than a question
data being smuggled out rather than a genuine information need
exfil_guard.on_suspicion: downgrade to ask (human y/N) or deny

web_search._note: Search-only, GATED. The local model emits capability-request{web_search, query}; the ORCHESTRATOR (cloud Claude) screens the query for exfiltration, runs it with its OWN WebSearch tool, and returns snippets via capability-result. The local model never searches directly and never fetches arbitrary URLs.

web_fetch._note: DEFERRED. Arbitrary-URL fetch is the strongest exfiltration + SSRF channel. Stays denied until a domain allowlist + SSRF/private-IP guard exist. 'Search only' was chosen; do not hand-fulfill a fetch.

bash._note: The local model has only read-only fs tools (aichat-config: fs_ls, fs_cat). Do not grant shell on its behalf.
